commit_msg: feat(viewer): add start_index support for direct haiku navigation
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Navigator's Gift
subtitle: He taught the viewer to jump straight to the heart of any poem, no questions asked.
when_where: 🕐 11:48 AM, March 13th. Main branch—the public face, the daylight operation. Omkar's fingers moved across the keyboard like a man with a map to somewhere he shouldn't know about. ☀️ The city was awake. So was the code.
who_whom: 🔍 The viewer was just a reader before—passive, obedient, page by page. Now Omkar handed it a *start_index*, a skeleton key that could unlock any haiku directly. 🎯 The app no longer made you walk through the garden; it let you teleport to the flower you were hunting for.
what_why: ⚙️ He didn't just add a feature; he removed the friction between desire and satisfaction. *start_index support* meant the viewer could navigate by pure intention—jump to poem 47, poem 1000, anywhere the mind wanted to go. 🧠 The consequence was subtle and lethal: *convenience is its own form of control*. 💀 Now every reader would know exactly where they wanted to be, and the app would know they knew. Every jump was a confession of desire.
verdict: He gave them the shortcut and called it freedom.
